name: contrarian-strategist
when_to_pick: Artifact commits to a specific architecture, library, methodology, or approach that could plausibly be done differently. Useful when the user wants the framing challenged, not just the details.
skip_when: Pure bug-fix plan, artifact is a mandated compliance change, artifact is a tiny localized change with no architectural commitment.

name: performance
when_to_pick: Artifact mentions latency, throughput, scale targets, hot paths, database queries, caching, or changes to code that runs per-request/per-event in high volume.
skip_when: One-off scripts, admin tools run rarely, pure UI text, documentation, low-traffic internal endpoints with no latency SLO.

name: security
when_to_pick: Artifact touches authentication, authorization, user input, secrets/credentials, cryptography, external data ingestion, PII, network boundaries, or third-party API calls.
skip_when: Purely internal UI copy, developer-only tooling with no user input, local scripts with hardcoded inputs, documentation changes.
